scores.materiality_reasoning: Assigned theater seating is a concrete social privilege with spatial/physical presence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific institution (the theater) and social setting.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Rank-based seating is a long-term benefit for the duration of one's senatorial career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Senator is a structural legal/social status in the Roman world.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes the social realities and seating ranks of his own Roman society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The anecdote uses a breach of the rule to prove its existence as a known fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Senators are defined by their political role, not specifically by where they sit in theaters.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from menial labor and transport is a physical/subsistence benefit.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad range of manual labor and service tasks in daily life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The condition of being a slave and performing labor is usually lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a legal birth-status requiring manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian depicts standard master-slave domestic relations of the 2nd century CE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a direct command illustrating an unremarkable social fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Performing menial labor for a master is inherently part of the definition of slavery.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to a legal name and legal personhood has direct material and legal consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a recurring religious/legal context regarding sacred naming.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The loss of name after hallowing is described as a permanent state ('thenceforth').
scores.immutability_reasoning: Religious initiation is a voluntary change that creates a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the actual contemporary practices of the Eleusinian Mysteries in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific legal charge based on a known religious statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an initiate does not inherently require losing one's name by definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from corporal punishment concerns bodily integrity and physical pain.
scores.generality_reasoning: Bodily integrity is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The lack of protection is a lifelong structural vulnerability of the slave status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status as a slave/worker is a hard-to-change legal or structural category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Depicts standard physical disciplinary practices of the Roman era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The detail about the 'back of many colours' is reported as an observable fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: While common, being whipped is not the formal definition of the group 'worker'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from corporal restraint and the stocks is a matter of physical freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Legal powers of detention cover a broad category of social control.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The restraint is tied to a specific procedure/trial episode.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group 'bezonian' or stranger is an episodic role in a single trial.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the legal powers of magistrates in Lucian's own time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Directly reports a magistrate's common procedural powers in a case.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a stranger does not tautologically mean being in chains.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A public statue is a symbolic honor with minor social capital side-effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a very specific form of recognition (the public square statue).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Statues are intended as permanent, lifelong, and posthumous honors.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status as a 'champion' is a durable social identity earned through skill.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian describes a statue visible in a contemporary city square.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites the physical existence of the statue as fact ('you know, in the square').
scores.tautology_reasoning: A champion is not defined as 'someone who has a statue'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Social recognition for style and a 'great name' are symbolic/prestige-based assets.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a recurring social role (literary personality) in elite circles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Literary reputation is a long-term asset once acquired.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'uneducated' is a changeable status, but education levels are durable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian discusses the standard educational values of the Second Sophistic.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is exhortative advice ('avoid and shun') rather than a report of law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The 'uneducated' are not defined by their lack of 'praise for style'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from social interaction and contact has direct material effects on life.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to specific health/marital conditions but recurring.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the duration of the illness/infertility episode.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The rule is applied based on sex (woman), which is immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the gendered social norms of the author's era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a specific wife's exclusion as a factual occurrence.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman does not tautologically mean being unapproachable when ill.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from derogatory labels is a purely symbolic/reputational resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a single linguistic/social category ('slattern').
scores.persistence_reasoning: Social labels of this type are long-term reputational features.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group is defined by sex, which is strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses linguistic usage contemporary to the author.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the restricted application of a word as a plain fact of language.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Femaleness does not define the word 'slattern', even if only women are called it.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical control and possession of a painting are tangible property rights.
scores.generality_reasoning: Refers to a specific professional right (withdrawal of art).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Control over one's creation is a long-term professional capacity.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (artist) is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Lucian is recounting a historical anecdote about Zeuxis (5th century BCE).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports Zeuxis' actions as historical fact/anecdote.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an artist does not inherently entail the right to reclaim work once exhibited.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The power to crown a king is a legal/political right with direct material power outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Constitutes a foundational mechanism for determining sovereign power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tied to the specific episode of victory/proclamation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (soldier) is durable but changeable through discharge or death.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes historical Hellenistic military practices long before Lucian.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the acclamation as a specific event in historical narrative.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Soldiers are not defined as 'people who choose the king'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A public salary is a direct transfer of wealth/money.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad right spanning many administrative roles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Salary persists as long as the administrative office is held.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status as a public official is an office-based, changeable role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the real administrative structures of the Roman Empire.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Presents receiving empirical pay as a structural fact of public life.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Administrators are defined by their work, not by the source of their pay.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Fees/payment are direct material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular profession (rhetoric/teaching).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The ability to command fees is tied to a lifelong professional career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a sophist is a durable identity acquired through training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to the author's own professional career in the 2nd century.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the existence of these fees as biographical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Sophists are not defined as 'people who get high fees'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Temples and statues are concrete, but their primary purpose here is symbolic honor.
scores.generality_reasoning: Imperial honors are a foundational axis of the Roman political system.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Posthumous cult and statues are intended to be permanent.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Status as Emperor is a terminal, structural political rank.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the honors paid to contemporary Roman Emperors.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A mix of factual description and idealized framing of 'rewards'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Emperors are defined by power, not specifically by receiving temples.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Initiation into the mysteries had significant direct social/legal status effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific institution (the Mysteries).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Religious initiation is a permanent, lifelong status change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Foreigner/Citizen status is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the historical case of Anacharsis in Classical Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the exclusion and exception as historical/traditional fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A barbarian is not defined by their lack of initiation into Greek mysteries.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to own land is a primary material property entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Land ownership is a foundational axis of ancient society.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Land ownership is typically structural and lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Foreigner/Metic status is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Relates to the status of a Scythian in Classical Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The description implies the fact of non-ownership through the small mound on public land.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A foreigner is defined by origin, not by landlessness.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legislative power has massive material effects on how a society is ordered.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political power to frame laws is a foundational axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right to participate in legislation was usually lifelong for citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is legal birth-status, very hard to leave.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the legislative power of citizens in Classical Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: An idealized description of citizen political capacity.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizens are defined by membership, not specifically the role of framing laws.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Influence over assemblies and reverence translate into direct political power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Public oratory and influence cover most major public affairs.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Aristocratic influence and family lineage are lifelong assets.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lineage and noble birth are strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the historical dynamics of Athenian leaders like Pericles.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Highly rhetorical and idealized description ('happiness... summed up in this one man').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Nobles are defined by birth, not by assembly reverence.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to vote in a jury determines property and life outcomes for others.
scores.generality_reasoning: Judicial power is a foundational axis of citizenship.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right to serve on juries was a permanent feature of citizen life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being an 'Athenian' is a legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Demades writes as a contemporary orator to the Athenian assembly.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim cites the law 'giving you the right' as a plain fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship is not defined as 'the right to vote in a jury'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public free speech is a foundational legal right with political consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Pertains to all major public and political discourse.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The professional right to the platform lasts as long as the career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (orator) is a durable social role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Demades describes his own contemporary professional environment.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the professional practice and its risks as a factual reality.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Orators are speakers, but 'speaking freely from the platform' is a specific right.
